entries.title: On Romans 16:5
entries.excerpt: Rom. XVI. 5 Salute my well-beloved Epenetus, who is the first-fruits of Achaia unto Christ. I think that many even of those who have the appearance of being extremely good men, hasten over this part of the Epistle as superfluous, and having no great weight in it. And I think that the same befalls them in regard to the genealogy that is in the Gospel. For because it is a catalogue of names, they think they cannot get any great good from it. Yet the gold founders' people are careful even about the little fragments; while these pass over even such great cakes of gold. That this then may not…
